args.code:
import json

# Get the business data
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:2']

# Analyze description fields to extract state information
descriptions = []
states_found = []

for business in business_data[:100]:  # Check first 100 records
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    if desc:
        descriptions.append(desc)
        # Look for state abbreviations in the description
        desc_upper = desc.upper()
        if ' CA' in desc_upper or ',CA' in desc_upper:
            states_found.append('CA')
        if ' MO' in desc_upper or ',MO' in desc_upper:
            states_found.append('MO')
        if ' ID' in desc_upper or ',ID' in desc_upper:
            states_found.append('ID')
        if ' FL' in desc_upper or ',FL' in desc_upper:
            states_found.append('FL')
        if ' IN' in desc_upper or ',IN' in desc_upper:
            states_found.append('IN')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_businesses': len(business_data),
    'sample_descriptions': descriptions[:5],
    'states_sample': states_found
}, indent=2))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all business data
business_file_path = var_functions.query_db:26

# Read the full file
with open(business_file_path, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

# Function to extract state
def extract_state(description):
    if not description:
        return None
    
    desc_upper = description.upper()
    
    us_states = ['AL', 'AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'FL', 'GA', 
                 'HI', 'ID', 'IL', 'IN', 'IA', 'KS', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD', 
                 'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ', 'NM', 
                 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC', 'SD', 
                 'TN', 'TX', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA', 'WV', 'WI', 'WY']
    
    for state in us_states:
        if f', {state}' in desc_upper or f' {state},' in desc_upper or f'. {state}' in desc_upper or f' IN {state}' in desc_upper:
            return state
    return None

# Aggregate by state
state_data = {}
for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    business_id = business.get('business_id', '')
    
    state = extract_state(desc)
    if state:
        if state not in state_data:
            state_data[state] = {'total_reviews': 0, 'business_ids': []}
        state_data[state]['total_reviews'] += review_count
        state_data[state]['business_ids'].append(business_id)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'record_count': len(business_data),
    'state_aggregates': state_data
}))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

result.preview: "Execution failed with exit code 1\nSyntaxError: invalid syntax"

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get business data
business_data = var_functions.query_db:26

# Extract state from description and aggregate review counts
state_totals = {}
state_businesses = {}

for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    business_id = business.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract state - look for pattern like ', XX' or ' XX,'
    state = None
    if desc:
        desc_upper = desc.upper()
        # Common state pattern: City, ST
        match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})', desc_upper)
        if match:
            state = match.group(1)
    
    if state:
        state_totals[state] = state_totals.get(state, 0) + review_count
        if state not in state_businesses:
            state_businesses[state] = []
        state_businesses[state].append(business_id)

# Find state with highest reviews
top_state = max(state_totals.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]) if state_totals else None

result = {
    'total_businesses': len(business_data),
    'state_totals': state_totals,
    'businesses_per_state': {k: len(v) for k, v in state_businesses.items()},
    'top_state': {
        'state': top_state[0],
        'total_reviews': top_state[1],
        'business_count': len(state_businesses[top_state[0]])
    } if top_state else None
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get business data
business_data = var_functions.query_db:26

# Extract state from description and aggregate review counts
state_totals = {}
state_businesses = {}

for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    business_id = business.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract state - look for pattern like ', XX' or ' XX,'
    state = None
    if desc:
        desc_upper = desc.upper()
        # Common state pattern: City, ST
        match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})', desc_upper)
        if match:
            state = match.group(1)
    
    if state:
        state_totals[state] = state_totals.get(state, 0) + review_count
        if state not in state_businesses:
            state_businesses[state] = []
        state_businesses[state].append(business_id)

# Find state with highest reviews
top_state = max(state_totals.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]) if state_totals else None

result = {
    'total_businesses': len(business_data),
    'state_totals': state_totals,
    'businesses_per_state': {k: len(v) for k, v in state_businesses.items()},
    'top_state': {
        'state': top_state[0],
        'total_reviews': top_state[1],
        'business_count': len(state_businesses[top_state[0]])
    } if top_state else None
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

SELECT r.rating, r.business_ref, b.description
FROM review r 
JOIN (
  SELECT unnest(ARRAY['businessid_47', 'businessid_49', 'businessid_88', 'businessid_41', 'businessid_33']) as business_id,
         unnest(ARRAY['businessref_47', 'businessref_49', 'businessref_88', 'businessref_41', 'businessref_33']) as business_ref,
         unnest(ARRAY['MO', 'CA', 'ID', 'IN', 'IN']) as state
) b ON r.business_ref = b.business_ref
WHERE b.state = 'MO'

exec_args.sql:
SELECT r.rating, r.business_ref, b.description
FROM review r 
JOIN (
  SELECT unnest(ARRAY['businessid_47', 'businessid_49', 'businessid_88', 'businessid_41', 'businessid_33']) as business_id,
         unnest(ARRAY['businessref_47', 'businessref_49', 'businessref_88', 'businessref_41', 'businessref_33']) as business_ref,
         unnest(ARRAY['MO', 'CA', 'ID', 'IN', 'IN']) as state
) b ON r.business_ref = b.business_ref
WHERE b.state = 'MO'

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json

# Get Missouri ratings
missouri_ratings_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:42')
missouri_ratings = [int(r['rating']) for r in missouri_ratings_data]

# Calculate average rating for Missouri
avg_rating_missouri = sum(missouri_ratings) / len(missouri_ratings) if missouri_ratings else 0

# Get state totals from business data
business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:26')

state_totals = {}
for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state
    state = None
    if 'CA' in desc:
        state = 'CA'
    elif 'MO' in desc:
        state = 'MO'
    elif 'ID' in desc:
        state = 'ID'
    elif 'FL' in desc:
        state = 'FL'
    elif 'IN' in desc:
        state = 'IN'
    
    if state:
        state_totals[state] = state_totals.get(state, 0) + review_count

# Find top state
top_state = max(state_totals.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])

result = {
    'top_state': top_state[0],
    'total_reviews_in_state': top_state[1],
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating_missouri, 2),
    'actual_reviews_found': len(missouri_ratings)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json

# Get Missouri ratings
missouri_ratings_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:42')
missouri_ratings = [int(r['rating']) for r in missouri_ratings_data]

# Calculate average rating for Missouri
avg_rating_missouri = sum(missouri_ratings) / len(missouri_ratings) if missouri_ratings else 0

# Get state totals from business data
business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:26')

state_totals = {}
for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state
    state = None
    if 'CA' in desc:
        state = 'CA'
    elif 'MO' in desc:
        state = 'MO'
    elif 'ID' in desc:
        state = 'ID'
    elif 'FL' in desc:
        state = 'FL'
    elif 'IN' in desc:
        state = 'IN'
    
    if state:
        state_totals[state] = state_totals.get(state, 0) + review_count

# Find top state
top_state = max(state_totals.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])

result = {
    'top_state': top_state[0],
    'total_reviews_in_state': top_state[1],
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating_missouri, 2),
    'actual_reviews_found': len(missouri_ratings)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json

# Get all ratings for Missouri businesses
missouri_ratings_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:56')
all_ratings = [int(r['rating']) for r in missouri_ratings_data]

# Calculate average rating
avg_rating_missouri = sum(all_ratings) / len(all_ratings) if all_ratings else 0

# Get Missouri businesses from the last query
missouri_businesses = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:54')
total_reviews_in_mo = sum(int(b['review_count']) for b in missouri_businesses)

result = {
    'state': 'Missouri (MO)',
    'total_review_count_from_businesses': total_reviews_in_mo,
    'total_actual_reviews_found': len(all_ratings),
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating_missouri, 2)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json

# Get all ratings for Missouri businesses
missouri_ratings_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:56')
all_ratings = [int(r['rating']) for r in missouri_ratings_data]

# Calculate average rating
avg_rating_missouri = sum(all_ratings) / len(all_ratings) if all_ratings else 0

# Get Missouri businesses from the last query
missouri_businesses = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:54')
total_reviews_in_mo = sum(int(b['review_count']) for b in missouri_businesses)

result = {
    'state': 'Missouri (MO)',
    'total_review_count_from_businesses': total_reviews_in_mo,
    'total_actual_reviews_found': len(all_ratings),
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating_missouri, 2)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all business data
business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:26')

# State extraction function
def extract_state(description):
    if not description:
        return None
    
    desc_upper = description.upper()
    
    # US state abbreviations
    us_states = ['AL', 'AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'FL', 'GA', 
                 'HI', 'ID', 'IL', 'IN', 'IA', 'KS', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD', 
                 'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ', 'NM', 
                 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC', 'SD', 
                 'TN', 'TX', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA', 'WV', 'WI', 'WY']
    
    # Look for pattern like City, ST
    parts = desc_upper.split(',')
    if len(parts) >= 2:
        after_comma = parts[1]
        words = after_comma.split()
        for word in words:
            if len(word) == 2 and word.isalpha() and word in us_states:
                return word
    
    return None

# Aggregate review counts by state
state_review_counts = {}
state_business_map = {}  # Maps state to list of business numbers
business_state_map = {}  # Maps business number to state

for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    business_id = business.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract business number
    business_num = business_id.replace('businessid_', '') if business_id else ''
    
    # Extract state
    state = extract_state(desc)
    
    if state and business_num:
        state_review_counts[state] = state_review_counts.get(state, 0) + review_count
        if state not in state_business_map:
            state_business_map[state] = []
        state_business_map[state].append(business_num)
        business_state_map[business_num] = state

top_state = max(state_review_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]) if state_review_counts else None

result = {
    'total_businesses': len(business_data),

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all business data
business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:26')

# State extraction function
def extract_state(description):
    if not description:
        return None
    
    desc_upper = description.upper()
    
    # US state abbreviations
    us_states = ['AL', 'AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'FL', 'GA', 
                 'HI', 'ID', 'IL', 'IN', 'IA', 'KS', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD', 
                 'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ', 'NM', 
                 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC', 'SD', 
                 'TN', 'TX', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA', 'WV', 'WI', 'WY']
    
    # Look for pattern like City, ST
    parts = desc_upper.split(',')
    if len(parts) >= 2:
        after_comma = parts[1]
        words = after_comma.split()
        for word in words:
            if len(word) == 2 and word.isalpha() and word in us_states:
                return word
    
    return None

# Aggregate review counts by state
state_review_counts = {}
state_business_map = {}  # Maps state to list of business numbers
business_state_map = {}  # Maps business number to state

for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    business_id = business.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract business number
    business_num = business_id.replace('businessid_', '') if business_id else ''
    
    # Extract state
    state = extract_state(desc)
    
    if state and business_num:
        state_review_counts[state] = state_review_counts.get(state, 0) + review_count
        if state not in state_business_map:
            state_business_map[state] = []
        state_business_map[state].append(business_num)
        business_state_map[business_num] = state

top_state = max(state_review_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]) if state_review_counts else None

result = {
    'total_businesses': len(business_data),
    'states_found': len(state_review_counts),
    'review_counts_by_state': state_review_counts,
    'top_state': {
        'state_code': top_state[0],
        'total_review_count': top_state[1],
        'businesses_in_state': len(state_business_map[top_state[0]])
    } if top_state else None,
    'business_ids_by_state': {k: v for k, v in state_business_map.items()}
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json

# Get Missouri ratings from earlier query
missouri_ratings_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:56')
missouri_ratings = [int(r['rating']) for r in missouri_ratings_data]

# Calculate average rating
average_rating = sum(missouri_ratings) / len(missouri_ratings) if missouri_ratings else 0

# Get all review data to verify no other state has more
all_ratings_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:64')

# Extract business numbers and group by state (based on earlier mapping)
# From earlier analysis: MO: [47, 64, 10], CA: [49], ID: [88], IN: [41, 33], FL: [41 is IN, not FL - wait]

# Based on business data analysis:
# businessid_49 -> CA (Goleta, CA)
# businessid_47 -> MO (St. Louis, MO)  
# businessid_88 -> ID (Boise, ID)
# businessid_41 -> FL (Saint Petersburg, FL)
# businessid_33 -> IN (Avon, IN)

state_mapping = {
    '49': 'CA',
    '47': 'MO', 
    '88': 'ID',
    '41': 'FL',
    '33': 'IN'
}

# Group ratings by state
state_ratings = {}
for rating_record in all_ratings_data:
    business_ref = rating_record['business_ref']  # e.g. 'businessref_47'
    rating = int(rating_record['rating'])
    
    # Extract business number
    business_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    state = state_mapping.get(business_num)
    
    if state:
        if state not in state_ratings:
            state_ratings[state] = []
        state_ratings[state].append(rating)

# Calculate review counts and averages by state
state_summary = {}
for state, ratings in state_ratings.items():
    state_summary[state] = {
        'review_count': len(ratings),
        'average_rating': sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    }

# Find state with highest review count
top_state = max(state_summary.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['review_count'])

result = {
    'top_state': top_state[0],
    'total_reviews': top_state[1]['review_count'],
    'average_rating': round(top_state[1]['average_rating'], 2),
    'all_states_summary': state_summary
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json

# Get Missouri ratings from earlier query
missouri_ratings_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:56')
missouri_ratings = [int(r['rating']) for r in missouri_ratings_data]

# Calculate average rating
average_rating = sum(missouri_ratings) / len(missouri_ratings) if missouri_ratings else 0

# Get all review data to verify no other state has more
all_ratings_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:64')

# Extract business numbers and group by state (based on earlier mapping)
# From earlier analysis: MO: [47, 64, 10], CA: [49], ID: [88], IN: [41, 33], FL: [41 is IN, not FL - wait]

# Based on business data analysis:
# businessid_49 -> CA (Goleta, CA)
# businessid_47 -> MO (St. Louis, MO)  
# businessid_88 -> ID (Boise, ID)
# businessid_41 -> FL (Saint Petersburg, FL)
# businessid_33 -> IN (Avon, IN)

state_mapping = {
    '49': 'CA',
    '47': 'MO', 
    '88': 'ID',
    '41': 'FL',
    '33': 'IN'
}

# Group ratings by state
state_ratings = {}
for rating_record in all_ratings_data:
    business_ref = rating_record['business_ref']  # e.g. 'businessref_47'
    rating = int(rating_record['rating'])
    
    # Extract business number
    business_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    state = state_mapping.get(business_num)
    
    if state:
        if state not in state_ratings:
            state_ratings[state] = []
        state_ratings[state].append(rating)

# Calculate review counts and averages by state
state_summary = {}
for state, ratings in state_ratings.items():
    state_summary[state] = {
        'review_count': len(ratings),
        'average_rating': sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    }

# Find state with highest review count
top_state = max(state_summary.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['review_count'])

result = {
    'top_state': top_state[0],
    'total_reviews': top_state[1]['review_count'],
    'average_rating': round(top_state[1]['average_rating'], 2),
    'all_states_summary': state_summary
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
